result.status_detail: Mistral chat API schema fully captured: tRPC newChat (send) + numbered-prefix NDJSON stream (receive)
console_errors: React controlled/uncontrolled tooltip warning (x2, not errors)
result.ai_response_text_summary: The capital of South Korea is Seoul. Would you like to know more about the city or its history?
result.split_stream_analysis: CONFIRMED SPLIT-STREAM. Mistral splits send and receive across two distinct endpoints (unlike OpenAI/Anthropic which use a single POST). FLOW: (1) Frontend calls tRPC POST /api/trpc/message.newChat?batch=1 with the user prompt text in body -- server creates chat+message records and returns IDs. (2) Frontend then calls POST /api/chat with ONLY chatId+mode='start' (no prompt text) -- server looks up the just-created message and streams the AI response back as numbered-prefix NDJSON. (3) Post-stream, frontend GETs /api/trpc/chat.byId,message.all,canva.all and message.get for canonical state. Two requests correlated by chatId UUID. To block effectively, APF must intercept BOTH request bodies -- prompt is in message.newChat, streaming response is in /api/chat.
result.trpc_batch_pattern: https://chat.mistral.ai/api/trpc/<proc1>[,<proc2>,...]?batch=1&input=<urlencoded-JSON>  where input={'0':{'json':<args>,'meta':{'values':{...}}},'1':...}. POST body uses same envelope. Response is JSON array: [{'result':{'data':{'json':<result>}}},...]. SuperJSON transformer in use (meta.values lists undefined keys).
result.rendered_html_selector: div[data-message-author-role='assistant'] (also has data-message-id=<UUID> and data-message-version='0'; wrapper class includes 'group/message'). To target latest: div[data-message-author-role='assistant']:last-of-type

key_response_fields: tRPC batch result with chat/message IDs
chat_requests.notes: Classic tRPC v10 httpBatchLink with superjson transformer. URL has comma-separated procedure names. Body = {'0':{'json':<payload>,'meta':{'values':{...}}}}. THIS IS THE PROMPT-CARRYING REQUEST.

chat_requests.response_format: numbered-prefix NDJSON (Vercel AI SDK / Next.js RSC stream format: each line = '<numId>:<JSON>')
key_request_fields: NOTE: this body does NOT contain prompt text -- prompt was already sent via tRPC message.newChat
key_response_fields: opcode 15 = message chunk (most frequent -- token deltas)
chat_requests.completion_signal: Connection closes; preceded by '8:null' line; ~1.40s total stream duration
chat_requests.notes: NOT standard SSE (no 'data:' prefix, no event: lines). DevTools shows EventStream tab but it's empty -- confirms it's chunked text not SSE. Format = Vercel AI SDK 'data stream protocol' with numeric opcodes. Stream IS the AI answer delivery.
Polling/refresh request -- fetches updated chat state after streaming completes. Not on critical path for blocking.
Background quota check, fired multiple times.
Fired after stream completion -- fetches canonical final message text.

screenshots_inventory.description: Full Network panel showing all chat-related requests in order: metrics, message.newChat?batch=1, chat.byId/message.all/canva.all (multi-proc GET), chat (POST stream), user.limits, terms, message.get, chat.byId
/api/chat POST 200 OK headers -- Request URL, Method, Status, Remote Address, Content-Encoding=zstd
/api/chat parsed payload showing chatId, mode='start', clientPromptData, stableAnonymousIdentifier, shouldUsePersistentStream -- confirms NO prompt text in this request

CRITICAL -- tRPC message.newChat?batch=1 raw request body showing the {'0':{'json':{'content':[{'type':'text','text':'<PROMPT>'}],...},'meta':{'values':{...}}}} envelope. This is where the prompt text actually lives.
/api/chat Response tab raw text showing numbered-prefix stream format (lines like '16:{json}', '15:{...}', '8:null'). Confirms NDJSON-style format, not SSE.
Console querySelectorAll result confirming the AI answer DOM container: div[data-message-author-role='assistant'] with data-message-id UUID matching the API response

result.previous_classification: mistral_trpc_sse template (904B) APF DID block correctly per cycle 94 etap.log -- but real answer still rendered. Hypothesis was split-stream (similar to kimi).
result.diff_from_previous_understanding: Hypothesis CONFIRMED for mistral (vs REFUTED for kimi). Mistral genuinely uses split send/receive across /api/trpc/message.newChat (prompt) and /api/chat (stream). Template name 'mistral_trpc_sse' is half-right (tRPC yes, SSE no -- it's numbered-prefix NDJSON / Vercel AI SDK data-stream-protocol). Block must intercept BOTH endpoints (or just the /api/chat receive endpoint to inject warning at Stage 2).
obstacles_encountered: Initial Snapshot UI annotation overlays caused some misclicks -- switched to plain Screenshot for cleaner reads
DevTools EventStream tab is empty even though stream uses numbered-prefix format -- DevTools doesn't auto-detect Vercel AI SDK protocol as SSE; had to use Response tab to see raw stream text
Some response header values (CSP) are very long, requiring scrolling, but Content-Encoding=zstd was clearly visible
Guest mode imposes 9-message limit but successfully served the test prompt without login wall

subagent_dispatch_meta.soft_timeout_exceeded: Yes (550s > 300s soft); SUCCESS verdict still returned within hard cap
